pending.body:
finding_summary: Missing historical `BeadFailed` events are never repaired once a newer attempt exists, so same-`run_id` retries can undercount failures and delay `NeedsOperator` escalation.
affected_files: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs`
file_refs: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:167-176`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:209-231`, `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:559-567`
why: `failure_attempt_number()` marks a stale attempt as `already_recorded` as soon as a failed lineage row exists, even if its `BeadFailed` journal event is missing. When a newer attempt exists, `reconcile_failure_sync()` returns early from the stale-replay branch before calling `repair_task_run_with_disposition()`, so the missing historical event is never backfilled. For resumed retries that reuse `run_id` and collapse older lineage rows, future retry counts then rely on the shortened journal history and can miss a prior failure.
remediation_hint: Before returning on a stale replay, explicitly repair a missing historical `BeadFailed` event for the exact `(project_id, run_id, started_at)` attempt, even when a newer attempt already exists.

finding_summary: Resumed attempts still bind failure metadata to the last `RunFailed` with the same `run_id`, so a retry whose current `run_failed` record is missing can be reconciled with the previous attempt's timestamp and error message instead of failing closed.
affected_files: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs`, `src/cli/run.rs`
file_refs: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/daemon_loop.rs:2119-2152`, `src/cli/run.rs:1273-1299`, `src/cli/run.rs:1493-1508`
why: both code paths look up terminal failure state by `run_id` only. Resumed attempts reuse `run_id`, so after `run_resumed` a stale older `RunFailed` still satisfies the lookup if the new attempt crashes before its own durable `RunFailed` is written. The newer attempt is then recorded with stale diagnostics, and `failed_at` can even precede the bound `started_at`.
remediation_hint: Match terminal failure events to the exact attempt boundary, not just `run_id` — for example by rejecting `RunFailed` events that predate the resolved `started_at` / latest `RunResumed`, and failing closed when no terminal event exists for the current attempt.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 8 -> Plan and Implement
